categories.name: Credential Leakage
categories.description: Exposed credentials, API keys, secrets, and authentication tokens

patterns.name: Environment Files
patterns.intent: Discover exposed .env files with database credentials and API keys
patterns.legalBoundary: View only publicly indexed content. Do not use discovered credentials.

patterns.name: Private Keys
patterns.intent: Find exposed SSH/TLS private keys in public repositories or web servers

compiledExamples.google: "BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY" OR "BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY" OR "BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY"
compiledExamples.bing: "BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY" OR "BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY"

patterns.legalBoundary: Report exposed keys via responsible disclosure. Never use for authentication.

patterns.name: SQL Database Dumps
patterns.intent: Locate database exports containing user tables with credentials

compiledExamples.google: filetype:sql "INSERT INTO" (password OR passwd OR pwd)

patterns.legalBoundary: Never download or access exposed databases. Report to affected organization.

patterns.name: AWS Credentials
patterns.intent: Find exposed AWS access keys and secret tokens
patterns.legalBoundary: AWS credential abuse is a federal crime. Report via AWS abuse form.

patterns.name: Git Credential Files
patterns.intent: Find exposed .git-credentials or credential helpers
patterns.legalBoundary: Exposed git credentials enable repository compromise. Report to maintainers.

patterns.name: Credentials in Logs
patterns.intent: Find accidentally logged passwords in debug/application logs
patterns.legalBoundary: Log files may contain PII. Handle with appropriate data protection.

categories.name: Open Directories
categories.description: Exposed directory listings revealing file structures and sensitive content

patterns.name: Apache/Nginx Directory Listings
patterns.intent: Find default directory listings exposing file structures
patterns.legalBoundary: Viewing open directories is generally permitted. Downloading protected content is not.

patterns.name: Backup Directories
patterns.intent: Find exposed backup folders with sensitive archives

compiledExamples.google: intitle:"index of" (backup OR backups OR bak)

patterns.legalBoundary: Backup files often contain complete database dumps. Access may violate CFAA.

patterns.name: Exposed .git Directories
patterns.intent: Find publicly accessible .git folders enabling source code recovery
patterns.legalBoundary: Exposed .git enables full repo reconstruction. May contain secrets in history.

patterns.name: Upload Directories
patterns.intent: Find exposed upload folders potentially containing user-submitted content

compiledExamples.google: intitle:"index of" (inurl:/uploads/ OR inurl:/upload/ OR inurl:/files/)

patterns.legalBoundary: Upload directories may contain private user data. PII protection laws apply.

patterns.name: WordPress Content Directories
patterns.intent: Find exposed wp-content directories with plugins, themes, and uploads
patterns.legalBoundary: WordPress content may reveal vulnerable plugins. Exploitation without consent is illegal.

categories.name: Configuration Files
categories.description: Exposed web server, application, and infrastructure configuration files

patterns.name: ASP.NET web.config
patterns.intent: Find exposed IIS/ASP.NET configuration files with connection strings
patterns.legalBoundary: web.config files contain database credentials and encryption keys.

patterns.name: Nginx Configuration
patterns.intent: Find exposed nginx.conf revealing server architecture
patterns.legalBoundary: Server configs reveal internal paths, upstream servers, and security settings.

patterns.name: PHP Configuration
patterns.intent: Find exposed php.ini files revealing PHP security settings
patterns.legalBoundary: PHP configs reveal security configurations and potential weaknesses.

patterns.name: Docker Compose Files
patterns.intent: Find exposed docker-compose.yml with service configurations
patterns.legalBoundary: Docker configs expose service architecture, ports, and environment variables.

patterns.name: Kubernetes Configs
patterns.intent: Find exposed kubeconfig files with cluster credentials
patterns.legalBoundary: Kubeconfig provides full cluster access. Extremely high risk.

patterns.name: Terraform State Files
patterns.intent: Find exposed terraform.tfstate with infrastructure secrets
patterns.legalBoundary: Terraform state contains all resource configurations and secrets in plaintext.

categories.name: Backup Artifacts
categories.description: Database dumps, archive files, and backup artifacts

patterns.name: SQL Backup Files
patterns.intent: Find database backup files with complete data exports
patterns.legalBoundary: Database backups contain complete user data. Access is typically unauthorized.

patterns.name: Archive Backup Files
patterns.intent: Find compressed backup archives in open directories

compiledExamples.google: intitle:"index of" (".tar.gz" OR ".zip" OR ".rar" OR backup)

patterns.legalBoundary: Backup archives may contain complete site/database snapshots.

patterns.name: .bak Configuration Backups
patterns.intent: Find backup copies of configuration files
patterns.legalBoundary: Backup files often retain credentials from original configs.

patterns.name: .old Legacy Files
patterns.intent: Find old versions of files with potentially sensitive content
patterns.legalBoundary: Legacy files may have weaker security than current versions.

categories.name: Login Panels & Admin Interfaces
categories.description: Administrative interfaces, login pages, and management consoles

patterns.name: Generic Admin Panels
patterns.intent: Find administrative login pages for potential credential attacks
patterns.legalBoundary: Do not attempt authentication without authorization. Discovery only.

patterns.name: phpMyAdmin Interfaces
patterns.intent: Find exposed database management interfaces
patterns.legalBoundary: phpMyAdmin provides direct database access. High-value target for attackers.

patterns.name: CMS Admin Panels
patterns.intent: Find content management system administrative interfaces
patterns.legalBoundary: CMS panels are common attack vectors. Credential stuffing is illegal.

patterns.name: Network Device Consoles
patterns.intent: Find exposed router, firewall, and network device management interfaces

compiledExamples.google: intitle:RouterOS OR intitle:pfSense OR intitle:FortiGate OR inurl:/webvpn/

patterns.legalBoundary: Network devices control infrastructure. Unauthorized access is critical infrastructure attack.

patterns.name: Monitoring Dashboards
patterns.intent: Find exposed Grafana, Kibana, and monitoring interfaces
patterns.legalBoundary: Monitoring dashboards reveal infrastructure metrics and potential vulnerabilities.

categories.name: Public Document Exposure
categories.description: Sensitive documents, reports, and files indexed by search engines

patterns.name: Confidential PDF Documents
patterns.intent: Find PDF documents marked as confidential or internal

compiledExamples.google: filetype:pdf ("confidential" OR "internal only" OR "not for distribution")

patterns.legalBoundary: Documents marked confidential have an expectation of privacy. Access may be restricted.

patterns.name: Financial Documents
patterns.intent: Find financial reports, budgets, and accounting documents

compiledExamples.google: (filetype:xlsx OR filetype:xls) (budget OR salary OR revenue OR financial)

patterns.legalBoundary: Financial documents may contain material non-public information. Securities laws apply.

patterns.name: HR & Personnel Documents
patterns.intent: Find HR documents, employee records, and personnel files

compiledExamples.google: (filetype:pdf OR filetype:docx) ("employee handbook" OR "personnel file" OR ssn OR "performance review")

patterns.legalBoundary: HR documents contain PII. GDPR/CCPA violations may apply.

patterns.name: Legal Documents
patterns.intent: Find contracts, NDAs, and legal filings

compiledExamples.google: filetype:pdf ("non-disclosure agreement" OR nda OR "master service agreement")

patterns.legalBoundary: Legal documents reveal business relationships and obligations.

categories.name: Organization & Personnel Discovery
categories.description: Employee information, organizational structure, and contact details

patterns.name: Email Address Harvesting
patterns.intent: Collect organizational email addresses for reconnaissance
patterns.legalBoundary: Email harvesting for spam is illegal. OSINT collection requires legitimate purpose.

patterns.name: LinkedIn Employee Profiles
patterns.intent: Find employees on LinkedIn for organizational mapping
patterns.legalBoundary: LinkedIn data scraping violates ToS. Use for manual review only.

patterns.name: GitHub Commit Emails
patterns.intent: Find developer emails from public commit history
patterns.legalBoundary: Git commit emails are public. May reveal employee coding activities.

patterns.name: Organizational Directory
patterns.intent: Find employee directories and organizational charts

compiledExamples.google: site:TARGET.com (intitle:directory OR intitle:team OR intitle:staff OR intitle:"about us")

patterns.legalBoundary: Public organizational data. Useful for social engineering awareness.

patterns.name: Employee Resumes
patterns.intent: Find employee resumes revealing skills and internal systems
patterns.legalBoundary: Resumes contain PII. Collection should be for authorized purposes.

categories.name: Cloud Storage Exposure
categories.description: Misconfigured cloud storage buckets and CDN origins

patterns.name: AWS S3 Bucket Listings
patterns.intent: Find publicly accessible S3 buckets with sensitive content

compiledExamples.google: site:s3.amazonaws.com (intitle:"index of" OR backup OR private)

patterns.legalBoundary: S3 bucket access may violate CFAA even if misconfigured. Report to AWS.

patterns.name: Google Cloud Storage
patterns.intent: Find publicly accessible GCS buckets
patterns.legalBoundary: GCS access controls may be misconfigured. Report to project owner.

patterns.name: Azure Blob Storage
patterns.intent: Find publicly accessible Azure storage containers
patterns.legalBoundary: Azure blob access may require enumeration. Respect access controls.

patterns.name: Firebase Storage
patterns.intent: Find exposed Firebase storage instances
patterns.legalBoundary: Firebase often stores mobile app data. May contain user PII.

categories.name: Vulnerability Indicators
categories.description: Error messages, debug outputs, and vulnerability signatures

patterns.name: SQL Error Messages
patterns.intent: Find SQL injection indicators from error messages
patterns.legalBoundary: Error messages indicate potential SQLi. Do not attempt exploitation.

patterns.name: phpinfo() Pages
patterns.intent: Find exposed PHP configuration pages revealing server details
patterns.legalBoundary: phpinfo reveals server configuration and potential vulnerabilities.

patterns.name: Debug Mode Enabled
patterns.intent: Find applications running in debug mode with verbose errors

compiledExamples.google: "DEBUG = True" OR "debug mode" OR "stack trace" OR "Traceback (most recent call last)"

patterns.legalBoundary: Debug mode reveals application internals. Report to developers.

patterns.name: Server Status Pages
patterns.intent: Find exposed Apache/Nginx status pages

compiledExamples.google: intitle:"Apache Status" OR inurl:/server-status OR inurl:/nginx_status

patterns.legalBoundary: Status pages reveal active connections and internal IPs.
